title: Sour Spicy Zanzibari Tomato Stir-fry
description: A low-calorie-friendly and nostalgic Kenyan dish featuring cumin seeds, designed to be tender, bitter, and light with a sour-spicy kick.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a wok or skillet over medium heat.
Add cumin seeds and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add onions and garlic. Sauté until onions are translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Increase heat to high, add tomatoes with their juice (if using canned), red pepper flakes, and salt. Stir-fry for 5-7 minutes or until most of the liquid has evaporated.
Turn down heat to medium-low, add chopped cilantro if using, stirring well to combine.
Serve hot over steamed rice or as a side dish.
